halt_reason: Session unable to compute L''(E,1) to 200 decimal places, and unable to fetch the 2388-curve list from LMFDB.

blockers.detail: The active venv (.venv/Scripts/python.exe, Python 3.14.x) has only mpmath 1.3.0 and sympy 1.14.0. No sage, no cypari2, no gp/PARI on PATH. Computing L''(E,1) to 200 dp for an elliptic curve requires either (a) Dokchitser via Sage/PARI, or (b) a from-scratch Dokchitser implementation in mpmath including an a_p oracle (Schoof or naive point-count) for primes up to ~ N^{1/2} * (#dp); neither is feasible within session budget.

LMFDB API endpoint https://www.lmfdb.org/api/ec_curvedata/?rank=i2&_format=json now returns a Google reCAPTCHA challenge page (HTML) instead of JSON. Single-record queries (e.g. lmfdb_label=389.a1) still succeed, but bulk paginated fetch of 2388 curves is blocked.

Even when LMFDB returns a record, the stored special_value precision is bounded. For 389.a1 the JSON gives special_value.prec=133 bits (≈40 dp). LMFDB cannot supply L''(E,1)/2 at 200 dp for any curve; the requested precision is unattainable from cached data.

remediation_options: Install Sage (WSL or Sage 10.x Windows binary) and re-run with E.lseries().dokchitser(prec=665) for 200 dp.
Install cypari2/PARI 2.15+ in the venv and use ellL1 / lfun with realprecision=200.

Downgrade precision target to ~30 dp and use LMFDB cached special_value, real_period, sha_an, tamagawa_product, torsion plus locally recomputed regulator from heights[]; this is feasible without external L-engines but does not satisfy the 200-dp claim.
Mirror the LMFDB ec_curvedata + ec_mwbsd dumps via beta.lmfdb.org or the Postgres dump to bypass the reCAPTCHA gate.
